mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of Kubernetes clusters on AWS. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with seamless integration into AWS services. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services, including EKS. Ensure you have the latest version installed.

```bash

pip install awscli --upgrade --user

```

> Use this command to install or upgrade the AWS CLI.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node configuration.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your actual AWS account ID.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new EKS cluster, allowing kubectl to interact with it.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This sets up your kubeconfig for the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your EKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Set Up Monitoring and Autoscaling
steps.description:
This step involves setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your EKS cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

```

> This applies the cluster autoscaler configuration.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

```
commands: aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the EKS cluster to a new version.

```bash

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to Kubernetes version 1.31.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are running the new version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that the nodes are running the expected version.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Cleanup
upgrade.description:
Remove any deprecated resources or configurations that are no longer needed after the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment old-deployment-name

```

> Replace old-deployment-name with any deprecated deployments.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No cluster found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity in your availability zones'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means there are not enough resources available in the specified AZs.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in different availability zones or adjust the instance types.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'UnauthorizedOperation'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the IAM role does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IAM role has the required permissions for EKS operations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means the cluster is not running or kubeconfig is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'aws eks describe-cluster --name my-cluster' and verify kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy APIs.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to review the deprecated APIs and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, confirming the cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
